title: Result - Denmark
content:
Welcome to Denmark, a Scandinavian gem south of Norway and Sweden, with the world’s 16th longest coastline, perfect for water activities and beach holidays. Explore the world’s oldest monarchy, historic castles and fascinating Viking sites.
With a population of just 5.7 million, Denmark shines as a cultural hotspot, renowned for its design, architecture, green energy and cuisine. Boasting a high standard of living and a stable economy driven by renewable energy, agriculture and shipping, Denmark is a modern success story. While Danish is the official language, most Danes are fluent in English.

## Unique food tradition
Smørrebrød (buttered bread): an open-faced sandwich on rye bread topped with a variety of ingredients, including pickled herring, smoked salmon, roast beef or cold cuts.
Denmark enjoys one of the highest standards of living in the world and a stable economy thanks to the renewable energy sector, an advanced agricultural sector and maritime shipping.

### Local legend or myth
Danish Nisse: temperamental household spirits who protect farms but are quick to retaliate when angered.

### Language of the Land
The "stød," a glottal stop, is a distinctive feature of Danish pronunciation that can alter word meanings. For instance, "bønder" (farmers) and "bønder" (peasants) are pronounced differently due to the presence or absence of the stød.
islands make up Denmark, the southernmost Scandinavian country, including the largest islands Zealand, Funen and the North Jutlandic Island, along with the Jutland peninsula.
